data.title: Simón Bolívar's rare firearm to be auctioned in Bedford; eyes on Caracas. Bidding war expected.
data.description: A highly anticipated bidding war is forecasted to ensue for a legendary firearm linked to South American revolutionary Simón Bolívar, as global attention shifts towards Caracas. The ornate presentation flintlock, once owned by Bolívar, is set to be auctioned off at the upcoming event hosted by Rock Island Auction Company (RIAC). Experts speculate that the historic firearm could fetch a significant sum, potentially attracting collectors and enthusiasts alike. This auction, taking place in Bedford, Texas on August 8, 2025, is poised to draw attention from around the world as interest in this rare piece intensifies.

data.title: Compass Therapeutics (Nasdaq: CMPX) to present Q2 financial results and business update on Aug 11, 2025 at 8:00 a.m. ET.
data.description: On August 8, 2025, Compass Therapeutics, Inc. (Nasdaq: CMPX), a biopharmaceutical company in Boston, announced its plans to reveal financial results for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. The company, which focuses on oncology, is known for developing proprietary antibody-based therapeutics. A webcast and conference call will be held on Monday, August 11, 2025, at 8:00 a.m. ET. During this event, updates will be provided regarding the tovecimig Phase 2/3 study, clinical data for CTX-8371, and preclinical data for CTX-10726.

data.title: SEC's Project Crypto impacts crypto businesses as the regulator clarifies policies, encouraging institutional investments and IPOs. 📈
data.description: The SEC's recent clarification on crypto, liquid staking, and tokenization has sparked a positive regulatory shift. Institutional investors and IPOs are showing increased interest in response to this development.

data.title: Currency Group's Founder & Executive Chairman converting $54.6M in loans to equity, reinforcing capital structure.
data.description: Improving Capital Structure and Demonstrating Trust in ExpansionA recent move was made to enhance the capital structure, reflecting confidence in growth prospects. This adjustment is a significant step towards fortifying the financial foundation and demonstrating belief in future expansion initiatives. The decision underscores a strategic approach towards securing a stable financial position to support growth opportunities. This development highlights a strong commitment to bolstering the foundation for future endeavors and comes as a signal of trust in the potential for future growth.This strategic maneuver marks an important milestone in the company's journey towards sustaining and enhancing growth prospects. By fortifying the capital structure, the company is poised to capitalize on emerging opportunities and navigate potential challenges more effectively. The focus on strengthening the financial framework showcases a proactive stance towards ensuring long-term sustainability and resilience. This move is expected to have a notable impact on the company's ability to pursue growth strategies and enhance shareholder value.Overall, the decision to enhance the capital structure represents a forward-looking approach aimed at positioning the company for future success. It signifies a strong belief in the growth trajectory and sets the stage for sustained development. As the business landscape continues to evolve, having a robust capital structure will be instrumental in driving innovation, expansion, and overall success. This move reflects a clear commitment to building a solid foundation for future growth and prosperity, underlining the company's dedication to creating long-term value for all stakeholders.

data.title: Taiga Building Products Ltd. reported increased sales of $441.0 million in Q2 2025 with a 3% growth compared to last year. Marginal increases seen in gross margin, net earnings, and EBITDA. Full details on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Taiga Building Products Ltd. reported its financial results for the second quarter ended June 30, 2025. Sales for this quarter were $441.0 million compared to $427.8 million in the previous year, a 3% increase. Gross margin rose to $49.2 million with a margin of 11.2%, higher than last year's 10.4%. Net earnings were $15.1 million, up from $13.9 million due to increased gross margin dollars. EBITDA for this quarter was $23.5 million, higher than last year's $22.7 million. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, net sales were $840.9 million, up from $821.5 million. Gross margin increased to $86.7 million, but net earnings decreased to $24.9 million from $26.7 million primarily due to higher expenses. EBITDA for the six months was $40.3 million compared to $42.5 million last year.

data.title: Palatin Technologies, Inc. announces 1-for-50 reverse stock split on August 8, 2025.
data.description: On August 8, 2025, Palatin Technologies, Inc. (OTCQB: PTNT) revealed a plan for a reverse stock split at a 1-for-50 ratio, set to take effect at 5:00 p.m. EDT on the same day in Princeton, N.J.

data.title: Madison Liquidators introduces The Ultimate L-Shaped Desk Guide for office inspiration and planning.(Words: 88)
data.description: In Madison, Wisconsin on August 8, 2025, Madison Liquidators, a top provider of high-quality office furniture, has unveiled The Ultimate L-Shaped Desk Guide. This comprehensive resource aims to assist individuals and businesses in selecting, customizing, and enhancing L-shaped desks for their workspace needs.

data.title: Euro strengthens, Fed likely to cut rates in September. Stocks fell after poor payroll data, only 73,000 jobs added, US dollar index dropped by over 1.5%. Key news next week includes Bank of England's anticipated rate cut.
data.description: The market last week was volatile but closed positively after initial weakness. Central banks held their rates: Bank of Canada at 2.75%, Bank of Japan at 0.50%, and the Fed at 4.50%. There were two dissents within the Fed for cutting rates for the first time since 1993. Stock sold off due to a 2.8% core PCE index reading and an abysmal non-farm payroll on Friday, with only 73,000 jobs added. Trump fired the Bureau of Labor Statistics Commissioner for supposedly rigging data. US dollar fell by over 1.5%, while yen and franc rallied. Commodity currencies like the Australian dollar weakened. Next week's highlights include Bank of England's anticipated rate cut and key news on CPI, ISM PMI, Unemployment, and GBP developments.

data.title: Stocks rebound on Wall Street Monday. Markets optimistic on Fed rate cuts (88% chance in Sept, 62% in Oct). Nasdaq up 1.8%, S&P 500 up 1.4%. Trump threatens tariffs on India. Palantir (NASDAQ: PLTR) up 3.7% ahead of earnings. U.S. dollar steady after 1.4% drop.
data.description: Monday saw a strong rebound in U.S. stocks following Friday's decline driven by concerns about a weakening labor market. The SPY is showing strength today. Investors turned optimistic about potential Federal Reserve rate cuts, with an 88% chance of a 25-basis-point cut in September and a 62% probability of another cut in October. The Nasdaq 100 rose by 1.8%, offsetting a 2% loss from Friday, while the S&P 500 recovered by 1.4% after a 1.6% drop. President Trump threatened more tariffs on India for allegedly profiting from reselling discounted Russian oil globally. Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR), the top-performing stock in the S&P 500 this year, gained 3.7% before its earnings report release. The U.S. dollar stabilized after a 1.4% decline on Friday, its largest drop recently. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.
